# terrible_cisco_dnac_compliance_device_by_id_info (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `category` (String) Category query parameter. Category can have any value among 'INTENT', 'RUNNING_CONFIG' , 'IMAGE' , 'PSIRT' , 'DESIGN_OOD' , 'EOX' , 'NETWORK_SETTINGS'.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `complianceType` (String) ComplianceType query parameter. Specify "Compliance type(s)" separated by commas. The Compliance type can be 'APPLICATION_VISIBILITY', 'EOX', 'FABRIC', 'IMAGE', 'NETWORK_PROFILE', 'NETWORK_SETTINGS', 'PSIRT', 'RUNNING_CONFIG', 'WORKFLOW'.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `deviceUuid` (String) DeviceUuid path parameter. Device Id.
- `diffList` (Boolean) DiffList query parameter. Diff list pass true to fetch the diff list.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `headers` (String) Additional headers.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `remediationSupported` (Boolean) RemediationSupported query parameter. The 'remediationSupported' parameter can be set to 'true' or 'false'. The result will be a combination of both values if it is not provided.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `status` (String) Status query parameter. 'COMPLIANT', 'NON_COMPLIANT', 'ERROR', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'NOT_APPLICABLE', 'NOT_AVAILABLE', 'WARNING', 'REMEDIATION_IN_PROGRESS' can be the value of the compliance 'status' parameter. COMPLIANT Device currently meets the compliance requirements. NON_COMPLIANT One of the compliance requirements like Software Image, PSIRT, Network Profile, Startup vs Running, etc. Are not met. ERROR Compliance is unable to compute status due to underlying errors. IN_PROGRESS Compliance check is in progress for the device. NOT_APPLICABLE Device is not supported for compliance, or minimum license requirement is not met. NOT_AVAILABLE Compliance is not available for the device. COMPLIANT_WARNING The device is compliant with warning if the last date of support is nearing. REMEDIATION_IN_PROGRESS Compliance remediation is in progress for the device.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `dnac_response` (String) A dictionary or list with the response returned by the Cisco DNAC Python SDK
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
